Compute summarize CDMs: Extract tidy summaries: bsvarPost also supports representative sign-restricted summaries restriction audits. median_target_*() selects stored draw closest posterior center. most_likely_admissible_*() ranks stored admissible draws reconstructed sign-restriction score available bsvarSIGNs. code: library(bsvarSIGNs) library(bsvarPost)  data(optimism)  sign_irf <- matrix(c(0, 1, rep(NA, 23)), 5, 5)  spec <- specify_bsvarSIGN$new(   optimism * 100,   p = 4,   sign_irf = sign_irf )  post <- estimate(spec, S = 100, thin = 1, show_progress = FALSE)  spec_alt <- specify_bsvarSIGN$new(   optimism * 100,   p = 2,   sign_irf = sign_irf ) post_alt <- estimate(spec_alt, S = 100, thin = 1, show_progress = FALSE) cdm_obj <- cdm(post, horizon = 12) summary(cdm_obj) plot(cdm_obj) irf_tbl <- tidy_irf(post, horizon = 12) cdm_tbl <- tidy_cdm(post, horizon = 12) fevd_tbl <- tidy_fevd(post, horizon = 12) rep_median <- median_target_irf(post, horizon = 12) rep_mla <- most_likely_admissible_irf(post, horizon = 12) summary(rep_median) summary(rep_mla)  audit_tbl <- restriction_audit(post) audit_tbl plot_restriction_audit(audit_tbl)  diag_tbl <- acceptance_diagnostics(post) diag_tbl  summary(diag_tbl)  compare_acceptance_diagnostics(p1 = post, p2 = post_alt) plot_acceptance_diagnostics(diag_tbl, metrics = c("effective_sample_size", "kernel_zero_share")) magnitude_audit(post, type = "irf", variable = 2, shock = 1, horizon = 0, relation = ">", value = 0)

title: Getting Started with bsvarPost
text: bsvarPost outputs already tidy tables, first reporting layer can stay simple: render directly knitr, gt, flextable, CSV. Use preset = "compact" want narrower publication-oriented column selection. practical rule thumb : use report_table(..., preset = "compact") paper tables use report_bundle() want natural table-plus-plot pair use publish_bsvar_plot() want family-aware styling without manual theme plumbing
code: cmp_tbl <- compare_irf(   p1 = post,   p2 = post_alt,   horizon = 8 )  report_table(cmp_tbl, preset = "compact", digits = 3) as_kable(cmp_tbl, caption = "Impulse-response comparison", digits = 3, preset = "compact") write_bsvar_csv(cmp_tbl, tempfile(fileext = ".csv"), preset = "compact")  if (requireNamespace("gt", quietly = TRUE)) {   as_gt(cmp_tbl, caption = "Impulse-response comparison", digits = 3, preset = "compact") }  if (requireNamespace("flextable", quietly = TRUE)) {   as_flextable(cmp_tbl, caption = "Impulse-response comparison", digits = 3, preset = "compact") }  bundle <- report_bundle(   cmp_tbl,   caption = "Impulse-response comparison",   digits = 3,   preset = "compact" )  bundle bundle$plot as_kable(bundle)  rep_bundle <- report_bundle(   median_target_irf(post, horizon = 8),   caption = "Representative impulse response" )  rep_bundle$plot as_kable(rep_bundle, preset = "compact")  diag_bundle <- report_bundle(   acceptance_diagnostics(post),   caption = "Acceptance diagnostics",   preset = "compact" )  diag_bundle$plot as_kable(diag_bundle)  joint_bundle <- report_bundle(   joint_hypothesis_irf(post, variable = 1, shock = 1, horizon = 0:2, relation = ">", value = 0),   caption = "Joint posterior statement",   preset = "compact" )  joint_bundle$plot as_kable(joint_bundle)  hd_bundle <- report_bundle(   tidy_hd_event(post, start = 1, end = 4),   preset = "compact" )  hd_bundle$caption hd_bundle$plot as_kable(hd_bundle)  publish_bsvar_plot(cmp_tbl, preset = "paper") publish_bsvar_plot(median_target_irf(post, horizon = 8), preset = "paper") publish_bsvar_plot(diag_tbl, preset = "slides")  `report_table()` also applies publication-facing column labels such as `Posterior probability`, `Median half-life`, and `Critical value`.

title: Getting Started with bsvarPost
text: final chunks reuse baseline-versus-alternative bsvars workflow much smaller posterior sample rendered document includes actual tables plots without making build heavy.
code: library(bsvars) library(bsvarPost) data(us_fiscal_lsuw) set.seed(11) demo_spec <- specify_bsvar$new(us_fiscal_lsuw, p = 1) #> The identification is set to the default option of lower-triangular structural matrix. demo_post <- estimate(demo_spec, S = 8, thin = 1, show_progress = FALSE) demo_spec_alt <- specify_bsvar$new(us_fiscal_lsuw, p = 3) #> The identification is set to the default option of lower-triangular structural matrix. demo_post_alt <- estimate(demo_spec_alt, S = 8, thin = 1, show_progress = FALSE) demo_cmp <- compare_irf(   p1 = demo_post,   p2 = demo_post_alt,   horizon = 3 ) demo_cmp_focus <- demo_cmp[   demo_cmp$variable == unique(demo_cmp$variable)[1] &     demo_cmp$shock == unique(demo_cmp$shock)[1],   ,   drop = FALSE ]  as_kable(demo_cmp_focus, preset = "compact", digits = 3) template_bsvar_plot(   ggplot2::autoplot(demo_cmp_focus),   family = "comparison",   preset = "paper" ) demo_cdm <- cdm(demo_post, horizon = 3) as_kable(summary(demo_cdm), preset = "compact", digits = 3) plot(demo_cdm) demo_rep <- median_target_irf(demo_post, horizon = 3) as_kable(summary(demo_rep), preset = "compact", digits = 3) publish_bsvar_plot(demo_rep, preset = "paper")

title: Post-Estimation Workflows in bsvarPost
text: sign-restricted analysis, pointwise posterior medians need correspond one coherent draw. bsvarPost therefore offers representative-model summaries. Use median_target_*() want stored draw closest posterior center. Use most_likely_admissible_*() want stored admissible draw highest reconstructed admissibility score. comparison post_alt useful changes dynamic specification leaving identifying sign restrictions unchanged.
code: library(bsvarSIGNs) library(bsvarPost)  data(optimism)  sign_irf <- matrix(c(0, 1, rep(NA, 23)), 5, 5)  spec <- specify_bsvarSIGN$new(   optimism * 100,   p = 4,   sign_irf = sign_irf )  post <- estimate(spec, S = 100, thin = 1, show_progress = FALSE)  spec_alt <- specify_bsvarSIGN$new(   optimism * 100,   p = 2,   sign_irf = sign_irf )  post_alt <- estimate(spec_alt, S = 100, thin = 1, show_progress = FALSE)  rep_median <- median_target_irf(post, horizon = 12) rep_mla <- most_likely_admissible_irf(post, horizon = 12)  summary(rep_median) summary(rep_mla) plot(rep_median)

code: library(bsvarSIGNs) #> Loading required package: RcppArmadillo #> Loading required package: bsvars library(bsvarPost) data(optimism) sign_irf_demo <- matrix(c(0, 1, rep(NA, 23)), 5, 5) set.seed(12) demo_spec <- specify_bsvarSIGN$new(   optimism * 100,   p = 2,   sign_irf = sign_irf_demo ) demo_post <- estimate(demo_spec, S = 5, thin = 1, show_progress = FALSE) demo_diag <- acceptance_diagnostics(demo_post) #> Argument standardise is forcibly set to FALSE due to zero restrictions imposed on the diagonal element(s) of the on-impact impulse response matrix. demo_rep <- median_target_irf(demo_post, horizon = 3) #> Argument standardise is forcibly set to FALSE due to zero restrictions imposed on the diagonal element(s) of the on-impact impulse response matrix. demo_audit <- restriction_audit(demo_post) #> Argument standardise is forcibly set to FALSE due to zero restrictions imposed on the diagonal element(s) of the on-impact impulse response matrix. demo_hd <- tidy_hd_event(demo_post, start = 1, end = 2)  as_kable(report_bundle(demo_diag, preset = "compact")) publish_bsvar_plot(demo_diag, preset = "paper") #> Warning: Using alpha for a discrete variable is not advised. as_kable(summary(demo_rep), preset = "compact", digits = 3) publish_bsvar_plot(demo_rep, preset = "paper") as_kable(demo_audit, preset = "compact", digits = 3) plot_restriction_audit(demo_audit) as_kable(demo_hd, preset = "compact", digits = 3) plot_hd_event(demo_post, start = 1, end = 2) #> `geom_line()`: Each group consists of only one observation. #> ℹ Do you need to adjust the group aesthetic? title: bsvarPost 0.2.0
text: Added representative-model selection helpers IRFs CDMs: representative_irf(), representative_cdm(), median_target_*(), most_likely_admissible_*(). Added posterior hypothesis helpers: hypothesis_irf() hypothesis_cdm(). Added audit helpers: restriction_audit() magnitude_audit(). Added tidy_hd_event() shock_ranking() event-window historical decomposition summaries. Added peak_response() duration_response() posterior response-shape summaries. Added compare_restrictions() cross-model restriction-audit comparisons. Added compare_hd_event() cross-model event-window historical decomposition comparisons. Added compare_peak_response() compare_duration_response() cross-model response-shape comparisons. Added half_life_response() time_to_threshold() response-decay threshold-timing summaries. Added compare_half_life_response() compare_time_to_threshold() cross-model timing-summary comparisons. Added plot_hd_event() plot_shock_ranking() dedicated event-study plotting helpers. Added theme_bsvarpost() style_bsvar_plot() reusable publication-oriented plot styling. Added template_bsvar_plot() annotate_bsvar_plot() output-family templates publication annotations. Added acceptance_diagnostics() stored-draw admissibility diagnostics bsvarSIGNs. Added summary() print support acceptance_diagnostics() outputs. Added compare_acceptance_diagnostics() plot_acceptance_diagnostics() cross-model diagnostics workflows. Added simultaneous_irf(), simultaneous_cdm(), joint_hypothesis_irf(), joint_hypothesis_cdm() first-pass joint inference support. Added plot_simultaneous() plot_joint_hypothesis() direct visualisation simultaneous bands joint posterior probability statements. Added plot_hypothesis() plot_restriction_audit() dedicated plotting helpers posterior statements restriction audits. Updated README vignette comparison examples use genuinely different model specifications instead self-comparisons. Added plot_compare_response() plot_compare_restrictions() dedicated visualisation comparison-table outputs. Added as_kable(), as_gt(), as_flextable(), write_bsvar_csv() first-pass reporting export helpers tidy bsvarPost tables. Added report_bundle() first-pass plot--table bundle publication workflows. Added report_table() compact/default reporting presets publication-oriented table layouts. Extended report_bundle() support representative-response acceptance-diagnostics workflows. Extended report_bundle() support simultaneous-band joint-hypothesis outputs. Extended report_bundle() support event-study tables object-family default captions. Added publish_bsvar_plot() family-aware publication templates across comparison, representative, diagnostics, event-study, joint-inference outputs. Added publication-facing report-table labels short family-specific subtitles representative, diagnostics, event-study, simultaneous-band, joint-hypothesis plots. Expanded vignettes clearer interpretation notes, non-trivial comparison examples, lightweight rendered table/plot showcases. Added pkgdown site scaffolding, including site configuration, homepage content, reference grouping, GitHub Pages deployment workflow. Added second vignette, Post-Estimation Workflows bsvarPost, separate workflow/methodology documentation getting-started material. Added restriction constructor helpers IRF, structural, narrative audits.

title: bsvarPost 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Added cross-package cdm() support posterior objects bsvars bsvarSIGNs. Added PosteriorCDM summary plotting methods. Added tidy extractors impulse responses, cumulative dynamic multipliers, variance decompositions, shocks, historical decompositions, forecasts. Added comparison helpers IRFs, CDMs, FEVDs, forecasts. Added ggplot2 autoplot support tidy comparison outputs. Added bridge helpers APRScenario-style forecast tables. Added optional tsibble conversion tidy outputs.
